title: LSB first registers (e.g. [0:N-1]) leads to incorrect logic

features.name: LSB-first packed array in procedural shift-left
features.description: Code should include a procedural shift-left operation on an unpacked array element where the packed dimension uses LSB-first indexing (ascending range [0:N-1])
features.snippet:
reg [0:DATADEPTH-1] data1 [DATAWIDTH-1:0];
always @(posedge CLK) data1[i] <= {SI[i], data1[i][0:DATADEPTH-2]};

features.name: bit_select on LSB-first unpacked array element
features.description: Code should include a bit-select operation on an unpacked array element where the packed dimension uses LSB-first indexing

features.name: unpacked array with LSB-first packed dimension
features.description: Code should include an unpacked array declaration where the packed array dimension uses LSB-first indexing (range [0:N-1] instead of [N-1:0])
